linear_context: SLM-138 adversarial audit ledger; direct successor to SLM-237 (RSC-A01)
note: Makes the final recursion depth's inclusion in the deep-supervision auxiliary term an explicit, named, validated choice instead of an unstated accident. Bounded 5-arm factorial (deterministic fixture + bounded real-corpus smoke) validates semantics/scale/telemetry only -- no quality or LOTUS-transfer claim, no promotion, no GPU campaign. That is SLM-233's job.

verified_ambiguity.evidence: SharedRecursiveDenoiserTower.recursive_outputs (src/slm_training/models/recursive_denoiser.py) sets final_logits = depth_logits[-1] and result['logits'] = final_logits -- the same tensor object, not a recomputation.
verified_ambiguity.consequence: The primary mask/reconstruction loss (computed from `logits`) and a historical all-depth auxiliary term (computed from every depth_logits[d] including d=-1) both differentiate through the identical final-depth forward pass. Pre-SLM-238, no config field, telemetry field, or documentation named this double path.

modes.off: primary final reconstruction only, no auxiliary term
modes.intermediate_only: auxiliary weights cover depths 0..R-2 exactly; the final depth is structurally never indexed by the auxiliary loop, let alone differentiated through
modes.all_depths: auxiliary weights cover depths 0..R-1 exactly; the final depth is deliberately counted in both the primary and auxiliary terms
modes.legacy_all_depths: identical math/length-rule to all_depths; a reproduction-only label reserved for configs/checkpoints that predate this field (never the recommended choice for a new config)

implementation.validator_reused_not_forked: validate_recursive_depth_supervision (SLM-237, src/slm_training/models/twotower.py) was extended in place with mode/aux_weight parameters and an eligible-depth-range check; no second parser was added.

backward_compatibility.mechanism: resolve_recursive_depth_aux_mode(mode, weights): mode=None (the true dataclass default) resolves deterministically to 'off' when recursive_depth_supervision_weights is empty, or 'legacy_all_depths' when non-empty -- reproducing SLM-237's weighted-mean-over-every-depth objective byte-for-byte with the neutral aux_weight=1.0 default.
backward_compatibility.checkpoint_migration: migrate_recursive_depth_aux_config(raw_cfg) makes this an explicit, persisted value at TwoTowerModel.from_checkpoint load time rather than perpetually relying on the None-resolution; idempotent, non-mutating, tested directly.
backward_compatibility.verified: All 26 pre-existing SLM-237 tests in tests/test_models/test_recursive_denoiser.py pass unmodified except one telemetry-presence assertion (see tests_changed below), because the resolver reproduces identical math for every config that never touches the new fields.

implementation.telemetry_contract: All six fields are always populated (explicit 0.0/mode string when the aux term is off or disabled), never omitted -- a disabled run stays distinguishable from a missing-metrics bug. combined_training_loss = primary_final_reconstruction_loss + recursive_depth_supervision_loss exactly, by construction; recursive_intermediate_aux_loss + recursive_final_depth_aux_contribution == recursive_depth_supervision_loss exactly.

tests_changed.test_empty_tuple_valid_on_every_architec: Updated (not removed) to assert recursive_depth_supervision_loss == 0.0 explicitly present, rather than absent -- this is SLM-238's deliberate telemetry-contract change (required test #4: an explicit zero record, never an omitted field), not a regression.

covering: test #1: intermediate_only structurally never reads/differentiates the final depth's auxiliary path
test #2: all_depths includes the final contribution exactly once
test #3: primary final loss is numerically identical across modes for fixed logits/targets
test #4: aux_weight=0 is exact primary-only loss with an explicit zero telemetry record
test #5: invalid mode/weight-length/aux_weight combinations raise (parametrized + dedicated)
test #6: R=1 intermediate_only reduces to primary-only (empty weights) / raises on non-empty weights (documented contract)
test #7: checkpoint round-trip preserves mode+coefficient; migrate_recursive_depth_aux_config is deterministic and idempotent
test #8: generated decomposition sums reproduce the scalar training_loss return value exactly

recipes: deterministic 2-record synthetic fixture (HERO/CTA, shared with SLM-138/SLM-237)
bounded_factorial.preregistered_lambda_justification: Chosen a priori (not tuned post-hoc): the final recursion depth already receives full weight once via the primary reconstruction term, so a partial (0.3x) extra credit in all_depths mode avoids letting the auxiliary term dominate or swamp the primary gradient signal while remaining large enough to matter; also a simple, easy-to-reproduce round number for a first calibration pass.
observations: combined_training_loss reproduces the actual training_loss() return value exactly (max diff < 1e-4) in every arm -- test #8's property holds live, not just under unit test.
recursive_final_depth_aux_contribution is exactly 0.0 in every intermediate_only arm (B, D) and structurally absent from the per-depth loop -- never merely zero-weighted.

For a fixed weights-shape family (uniform, all >0), first/last-depth contribution ratio is ~1.25 in both all_depths arms (C, E), stable under the lambda rescale (C: 1.2522, E: 1.2526) as expected since aux_weight scales the whole term.

Per-depth gradient-norm/cosine (measured on a freshly-initialized synthetic isolated-tower batch, independent of aux mode by construction since the mode only rescales the aggregated loss post-forward) shows cosine similarity with the final depth's gradient direction rising monotonically toward 1.0 with depth (0.973 / 0.996 / 1.0 for depths 0/1/2) -- an architecture-level property of the shared recursion, invariant across all 5 arms; this is a sanity check that recursive_depth_aux_mode does not secretly alter tower forward mechanics, only the loss aggregation.

params_and_flops_unchanged_across_arms verified structurally: every arm builds from the identical TwoTowerConfig module-construction fields (only recursive_depth_supervision_weights/recursive_depth_aux_mode/recursive_depth_aux_weight differ, none of which affect nn.Module construction).

safety_smoke.reason: The official @openuidev/lang-core Node bridge failed in this sandbox with a pre-existing, unrelated NODE_OPTIONS incompatibility ('--import tsx is not allowed in NODE_OPTIONS'), confirmed present identically on the pre-SLM-238 tree via git stash -- not introduced by this change. scripts/run_rsc_a02_depth_aux_mode_factorial.py detects this via the same _quality_pipeline_ok() pattern as scripts/run_perf_matrix.py and reports bridge_healthy=false explicitly per arm rather than fabricating pass/fail numbers.
safety_smoke.not_a_quality_claim: This smoke was always intended as a non-gating safety diagnostic only; its unavailability here does not affect any RSC-A02 acceptance criterion, all of which are semantics/telemetry/decomposition properties verified directly by the 40-test suite and the objective-decomposition numbers above.

recommendation.rationale: The final recursion depth's output IS the model's actual prediction; supervising it a second time through the auxiliary term (all_depths) rewards or penalizes the same forward computation twice per step for no architecturally distinct reason, and inflates its effective learning-rate contribution relative to the intermediate depths it was meant to help train. intermediate_only cleanly separates concerns -- the final depth's supervision comes solely from the primary reconstruction loss (as it should, since that is the actual output), while a coefficient-controlled auxiliary term (recursive_depth_aux_weight, preregistered lambda=0.3 as a starting point) supervises only the genuinely intermediate representations, matching the standard deep-supervision rationale (auxiliary heads on intermediate representations, not a second vote on the final one) used elsewhere in the literature. all_depths/legacy_all_depths remain explicit, fully-supported controls -- this factorial's bounded scale cannot rule out that the seemingly-redundant double count helps in practice (e.g. by acting like an implicit tower-strength-weighted final-depth boost); that question is exactly SLM-233's job, not decided here.
recommendation.slm_233_control_matrix_paragraph: A future SLM-233 GPU campaign should treat recursive_depth_aux_mode as a first-class matrix axis crossed with recursive_steps and recursive_depth_aux_weight (a small preregistered lambda grid, e.g. {0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 1.0}), evaluated under this repo's full honest ship gates (not the bounded smoke here) on real held-out OpenUI corpora at a training scale sufficient to move the ship-gate metrics outside noise. The control set should include off (no aux term at all) as the byte-identical baseline, intermediate_only at the recommended lambda as the primary candidate, and all_depths/legacy_all_depths as an explicit alternative-hypothesis arm -- explicitly to test whether double-counting the final depth is a net positive despite this doc's a priori architectural objection, rather than assuming the recommendation above without evidence.

slm138_doc_annotation.target: docs/design/quality-experiment-matrix.md, ## RSC-A01 section (E303/V18 deep-supervision, the E303 row of the V18 SLM-138 table)
slm138_doc_annotation.change: Added a note (not a rewrite) that the pre-existing all-depth deep-supervision objective double-counts the final recursion depth (through both the primary reconstruction term and the auxiliary term) -- an ambiguity neither SLM-138 nor SLM-237 named -- and that SLM-238 (RSC-A02) makes this an explicit, versioned, tested choice. SLM-138's original wiring-only verdict and SLM-237's correctness-fix verdict are unchanged; this is an annotation, not a re-verdict.

non_goals_honored: No new latent slots or recurrence architecture.
No routing through a separate auxiliary decoder.
No production default change outside the versioned recursive path (recursive_depth_aux_mode defaults to off/legacy-preserving None, never a new always-on behavior).
No adoption decision -- that is SLM-233's job; this doc only recommends a default-candidate for that future campaign.
No quality or LOTUS-transfer claim from the bounded factorial -- calibration/semantics only.
deferred_to_follow_up: SLM-233's full GPU control-matrix campaign (out of scope for SLM-238 by design).

A bridge-healthy rerun of the syntax/structure/strict-semantic safety smoke -- this session's sandbox has a pre-existing, unrelated Node bridge NODE_OPTIONS incompatibility (confirmed present on the pre-SLM-238 tree too).
